meta.title: Bible
meta.note: Ages 11–13. All cards are daily situations; answers are grounded in Scripture (not verse memorization).

items.prompt: You're starting a new class and feel too shy to talk to anyone. How can confidence grow without pretending you're fearless?
items.verse: 2 Timothy 1:7 — God gave us a spirit of power, love, and a sound mind—not fear.
items.takeaway: Feeling shy is normal. Ask God for courage, then take one small step (say hi, sit near someone).
items.answer: Ask God for courage, then take one small brave step

items.prompt: There's a party or youth group with lots of new kids. Your stomach flips. What does God say about fear when you meet new people?
items.verse: Isaiah 41:10 — Do not fear, for I am with you… I will strengthen you.
items.takeaway: God goes with you into the room. You don't have to be the loudest—just present and kind.
items.answer: Don't let fear stop you — God is with you

items.prompt: You want to try out for a team/play but keep thinking "I'm not good enough." What truth fights that lie?
items.verse: Philippians 4:13 — I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me.
items.takeaway: Confidence isn't "I'm the best." It's "God helps me try."
items.answer: Try with Christ's strength — don't quit before you start

items.prompt: Someone laughs at your idea and you want to stay silent forever. How should you think about yourself?
items.verse: Psalm 139:14 — I am fearfully and wonderfully made.
items.takeaway: One person's joke doesn't erase how God made you.
items.answer: Remember you are wonderfully made — keep showing up

items.prompt: You're nervous to introduce yourself to a new neighbor kid. What's a brave, simple move?
items.verse: Joshua 1:9 — Be strong and courageous… God is with you wherever you go.
items.takeaway: Courage can be three words: "Hi, I'm __." God is with you in ordinary hellos.
items.answer: Be courageous — go say a friendly hello

items.prompt: You feel small compared to older teens who seem cooler. Does age mean you don't matter?
items.verse: 1 Timothy 4:12 — Don't let anyone look down on you because you are young.
items.takeaway: At 11–13 you can already lead with kindness, honesty, and courage.
items.answer: No — your youth doesn't make you unimportant

items.prompt: Before a hard conversation (apology, asking for help), fear freezes you. What can you do with that worry?
items.verse: 1 Peter 5:7 — Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you.
items.takeaway: Pray first, then speak calmly. God cares about the awkward moments too.
items.answer: Give the fear to God, then take the next step

items.prompt: You miss a shot / mess up a presentation and want to hide. How do you bounce back?
items.verse: Proverbs 24:16 — The righteous fall seven times and rise again.
items.takeaway: Confidence grows by getting back up, not by never failing.
items.answer: Get back up — falling isn't the end

items.prompt: Why bother being nice when some people are rude first?
items.verse: Luke 6:31 — Do to others as you would have them do to you.
items.takeaway: Kindness isn't a reward for perfect people—it's how Jesus asks us to live.
items.answer: Treat others the way you'd want to be treated

items.prompt: What's the big reason Christians should be kind even when nobody's watching?
items.verse: Matthew 22:39 — Love your neighbor as yourself.
items.takeaway: People matter to God—so how you treat them matters.
items.answer: Love your neighbor — people matter to God

items.prompt: Friends start using foul language to sound cool. What should come out of your mouth?
items.verse: Ephesians 4:29 — Do not let any unwholesome talk come out of your mouths.
items.takeaway: Dirty words aren't "just words"—they shape your heart and hurt your witness.
items.answer: Don't use foul language — speak what builds people up

items.prompt: You're mad and want to swear under your breath. What does God want instead?
items.verse: Colossians 3:8 — Rid yourselves of… filthy language from your lips.
items.takeaway: Anger is a feeling. Foul language is a choice you can refuse.
items.answer: Drop the filthy language — choose cleaner words

items.prompt: A classmate trips and kids laugh. You're about to make a mean joke too. Stop—why?
items.verse: Proverbs 18:21 — The tongue has the power of life and death.
items.takeaway: Mean jokes can stick in someone's mind for years. Choose life.
items.answer: Don't say mean things — words can crush or heal

items.prompt: Your sibling embarrasses you and you want to roast them in front of friends. What's better?
items.verse: Ephesians 4:32 — Be kind and compassionate… forgiving each other.
items.takeaway: Family aren't practice targets for insults.
items.answer: Be kind — don't roast them to look cool

items.prompt: Someone posts a mean comment about a kid. Your group wants you to pile on. What do you do?
items.verse: Romans 12:17-18 — Do not repay anyone evil for evil… live at peace.
items.takeaway: Silence or a kind stand is better than joining the pile-on.
items.answer: Don't pile on — refuse to repay meanness with meanness

items.prompt: Why does God care if you're "just teasing" someone smaller or quieter?
items.verse: Genesis 1:27 — God created people in his own image.
items.takeaway: Mocking a person mocks someone made in God's image.
items.answer: Every person is made in God's image — treat them with respect

items.prompt: You know a rumor is juicy. Should you pass it on?
items.verse: Proverbs 16:28 — A gossip separates close friends.
items.takeaway: If it would hurt you, don't spread it.
items.answer: No — don't gossip or pass mean rumors

items.prompt: A kid who was mean to you last month looks lonely today. What would Jesus-shaped kindness look like?
items.verse: Matthew 5:44 — Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you.
items.takeaway: Kindness can be a quiet invite—not pretending the past never happened.
items.answer: Be kind anyway — even to people who weren't kind first

items.prompt: Parents set a screen-time rule you hate. What's the right response?
items.verse: Ephesians 6:1 — Children, obey your parents in the Lord.
items.takeaway: You can share feelings calmly—and still obey the limit.
items.answer: Obey and honor them even when you disagree

items.prompt: Mom asks you to pause your game and help. You want to groan. What attitude fits?
items.verse: Philippians 2:14 — Do everything without grumbling or arguing.
items.takeaway: Helping at home is love in action, not a punishment.
items.answer: Help without grumbling

items.prompt: Dad is stressed and short with you. You want to snap back. What does honor look like?
items.verse: Exodus 20:12 — Honor your father and your mother.
items.takeaway: Honor doesn't mean parents are perfect. It means you don't add fuel.
items.answer: Respond with respect, not more heat

items.prompt: Your sibling ruins your game build. You want to scream. What does love look like?
items.verse: 1 Corinthians 13:4 — Love is patient, love is kind.
items.takeaway: Family love shows up in small annoying moments.
items.answer: Be patient and kind — even when it's annoying

items.prompt: You're still mad at a sibling for days and won't forgive. What does Jesus connect to forgiveness?
items.verse: Matthew 6:14-15 — If you forgive others, your Father will also forgive you.
items.takeaway: Forgiving isn't saying "it was fine." It's dropping the revenge score.
items.answer: Forgive them — don't hold a grudge

items.prompt: You promised to do a chore, then a better invite came. What about your word?
items.verse: Matthew 5:37 — Let your "Yes" be yes and your "No" be no.
items.takeaway: Keeping promises at home builds trust.
items.answer: Keep your yes — follow through at home

items.prompt: You're jealous a cousin got new gear and you didn't. What does God warn about envy?
items.verse: Exodus 20:17 — You shall not covet.
items.takeaway: Wanting stuff is normal. Letting envy sour family isn't.
items.answer: Don't covet — practice contentment and gladness for them

items.prompt: Family dinner is boring and you want to stare at your phone. What fits better?
items.verse: Romans 12:10 — Be devoted to one another in love. Honor one another.
items.takeaway: Putting the phone down is a way to honor people in front of you.
items.answer: Be present — honor family over the screen

items.prompt: Friends dare you to cheat "just once." What does honesty require?
items.verse: Proverbs 12:22 — The Lord delights in people who are trustworthy.
items.takeaway: A quick cheat costs character.
items.answer: Don't cheat — be trustworthy

items.prompt: A new student eats alone. You feel awkward saying hi. What does loving your neighbor look like?
items.verse: Matthew 22:39 — Love your neighbor as yourself.
items.takeaway: "Want to sit with us?" can change a whole day.
items.answer: Include them the way you'd want to be included

items.prompt: Your friend group starts excluding one kid. What would love do?
items.verse: Luke 6:31 — Do to others as you would have them do to you.
items.takeaway: If you'd hate being left out, don't help leave someone out.
items.answer: Don't join the exclusion — include

items.prompt: You know someone's being bullied but speaking up feels uncool. What does James say?
items.verse: James 4:17 — If you know the good you ought to do and don't do it, it is sin.
items.takeaway: Courage often looks like telling a trusted adult.
items.answer: Do the good you know — speak up

items.prompt: A teacher is strict and you want to give up. How should you work?
items.verse: Colossians 3:23 — Work with all your heart, as working for the Lord.
items.takeaway: You're practicing faithfulness, not only chasing a grade.
items.answer: Work hard as if for the Lord

items.prompt: You bombed a test and can't stop replaying it at night. What do with the worry?
items.verse: Philippians 4:6-7 — Don't be anxious… pray about everything.
items.takeaway: Pray, then make a plan. Worry alone doesn't help.
items.answer: Pray instead of only stressing

items.prompt: Friend group language is getting worse. What's the warning about company?
items.verse: 1 Corinthians 15:33 — Bad company corrupts good character.
items.takeaway: Friends pull you up or down. Choose wisely.
items.answer: Be careful — friends can pull you into wrong

items.prompt: You got caught in a lie about homework. Next step?
items.verse: Proverbs 28:13 — Whoever confesses and renounces their sins finds mercy.
items.takeaway: Hiding usually makes it worse. Own it and make it right.
items.answer: Confess, apologize, and tell the truth

items.prompt: Someone asks if you go to church and you feel embarrassed. What courage fits?
items.verse: Matthew 5:16 — Let your light shine before others.
items.takeaway: A honest, kind answer still shines—no speech required.
items.answer: Don't hide your faith — let your light shine

items.prompt: A teammate messes up and others trash-talk them. How do you use your tongue?
items.verse: Proverbs 18:21 — The tongue has the power of life and death.
items.takeaway: Encourage. Don't crush.
items.answer: Speak life — encourage, don't tear down

items.prompt: You've gamed for hours and snapped at family when they asked for help. What's off?
items.verse: 1 Corinthians 10:31 — Whatever you do, do it all for the glory of God.
items.takeaway: Fun is fine until it makes you selfish. Then pause.
items.answer: Stop and reset — free time should still honor God and people

items.prompt: Endless scrolling is stealing sleep and chores. What fruit of the Spirit do you need?
items.verse: Galatians 5:22-23 — The fruit of the Spirit includes self-control.
items.takeaway: You can enjoy screens and still put them down.
items.answer: Use self-control — set the phone down when it's time

items.prompt: You lose online and the chat fills with rage. What should come from you?
items.verse: Colossians 4:6 — Let your conversation be always full of grace.
items.takeaway: It's a game. Grace > insults.
items.answer: Speak with grace — don't rage in chat

items.prompt: Videos leave you angry, scared, or dirty inside. What should fill your mind?
items.verse: Philippians 4:8 — Think about what is true, noble, right, pure, lovely…
items.takeaway: What you watch shapes you. Choose better input.
items.answer: Fill your mind with what is good and pure

items.prompt: You compare your life to highlight reels online and feel worthless. What's true?
items.verse: Psalm 139:14 — You are fearfully and wonderfully made.
items.takeaway: Filters aren't the truth about you—or them.
items.answer: You are wonderfully made — don't let comparison decide your worth

items.prompt: A viral challenge looks wrong/dangerous. Friends say everyone is doing it. Guide?
items.verse: Romans 12:2 — Do not conform to the pattern of this world.
items.takeaway: Popular ≠ wise. You can say no.
items.answer: Don't just copy the crowd — choose what's right

items.prompt: Pressure to buy every new game/app update is stressing you. How to think about "enough"?
items.verse: Hebrews 13:5 — Be content with what you have.
items.takeaway: Contentment is freedom from always needing more.
items.answer: Practice contentment — you don't need every new thing

items.prompt: Someone was rude online and you want to blast them publicly. Better path?
items.verse: James 1:19 — Be quick to listen, slow to speak, slow to become angry.
items.takeaway: Slow down. Adults can help. Online revenge usually explodes.
items.answer: Slow down — don't fire back in anger

items.prompt: You'd rather stay in your room all weekend than go outside or try something new. Why go out and see more of the world?
items.verse: Genesis 1:31 — God saw all that he had made, and it was very good.
items.takeaway: God's world is bigger than a bedroom. Fresh air, people, and places wake up gratitude.
items.answer: Go out — enjoy God's good world instead of only staying inside

items.prompt: You're curious about how things work, other cultures, or nature—but friends call it "nerdy." Is curiosity good?
items.verse: Proverbs 2:3-6 — Cry out for insight… the Lord gives wisdom.
items.takeaway: God loves wise, curious minds. Asking good questions is a gift.
items.answer: Yes — be curious; seek wisdom and understanding

items.prompt: You keep saying "I'm bored" and only want screens. How can curiosity change that?
items.verse: Proverbs 18:15 — The heart of the discerning acquires knowledge.
items.takeaway: Try a walk, a book, a hobby, a conversation. Boredom can be a doorway.
items.answer: Get curious — learn, explore, try something new

items.prompt: Family wants a hike/park day; you want to stay home in bed. What fits a life of adventure with God?
items.verse: Psalm 19:1 — The heavens declare the glory of God.
items.takeaway: Creation is a free museum. Going out can refill your soul.
items.answer: Go out and notice God's world — don't only stay home

items.prompt: You keep delaying your shower until someone nags you. Why take care of your body earlier?
items.verse: 1 Corinthians 6:19-20 — Your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit… honor God with your body.
items.takeaway: Hygiene isn't vanity—it's stewardship and kindness to people around you.
items.answer: Take a shower early — honor God with your body

items.prompt: You smell / look messy and hope nobody notices. What's a better habit?
items.verse: Colossians 3:23 — Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart.
items.takeaway: Basic care (shower, clean clothes, teeth) is part of doing life well.
items.answer: Build the habit — clean up without waiting to be nagged

items.prompt: Morning rush: you skip shower "just today" again. What does diligence look like?
items.verse: Proverbs 6:6-8 — Consider the ant… it stores its provisions in summer.
items.takeaway: Little daily habits (shower, pack bag, sleep) beat last-minute panic.
items.answer: Be diligent — do small good habits early

items.prompt: You're scared to try a new sport, club, or trip because it's unfamiliar. What invitation is bigger than comfort?
items.verse: Matthew 28:19-20 — Go and make disciples… I am with you always.
items.takeaway: Faith isn't only staying safe at home—it's going, learning, meeting people with God.
items.answer: Step out — God is with you beyond your comfort zone

items.prompt: You only talk to the same two people and never meet anyone new. Why stretch?
items.verse: Hebrews 13:2 — Do not forget to show hospitality to strangers.
items.takeaway: New people aren't threats by default—they can become friends.
items.answer: Be open to new people — practice welcoming others

items.prompt: Sunday you're tired and want to skip gathering with church family. Why still go?
items.verse: Hebrews 10:24-25 — Do not give up meeting together… encourage one another.
items.takeaway: Church is people who help you follow Jesus—not only a building.
items.answer: Keep meeting with others — encourage and be encouraged

items.prompt: You stay up late on screens, then feel foggy and cranky. What wisdom fits?
items.verse: Psalm 127:2 — In vain you rise early and stay up late… he grants sleep to those he loves.
items.takeaway: Rest is a gift. Protect sleep so you can love people better tomorrow.
items.answer: Honor rest — don't let screens steal sleep

items.prompt: Why should you be nice even when it doesn't get likes or applause?
items.verse: Matthew 5:16 — Let your light shine… that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father.
items.takeaway: Kindness points to God, not to your popularity score.
items.answer: Be kind to honor God — not to get attention

items.prompt: You freeze when adults ask you a question in a group. How can you grow confidence?
items.verse: Jeremiah 1:7-8 — Do not say, "I am too young"… Do not be afraid… I am with you.
items.takeaway: Practice short answers. God can use your voice at any age.
items.answer: Don't hide behind "I'm too young" — speak with God beside you

items.prompt: You're about to text something sarcastic that will sting. What filter should you use?
items.verse: Ephesians 4:29 — Only what is helpful for building others up.
items.takeaway: If it won't build them up, don't send it.
items.answer: Don't send mean words — only what builds up

items.prompt: You say you want adventure someday, but refuse every small outing now. What's the first step?
items.verse: Luke 16:10 — Whoever is faithful with very little will also be faithful with much.
items.takeaway: Big adventures grow from small yeses: walk, museum, family trip, saying hi.
items.answer: Be faithful in small outings — say yes to little adventures

items.prompt: You leave towels, dirty clothes, and dishes for others. What does love look like at home?
items.verse: Philippians 2:4 — Look not only to your own interests, but also to the interests of others.
items.takeaway: Clean up, shower on time, help—home is a team.
items.answer: Think of others — clean up and help without being asked
